title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
description: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

title: How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
description: The premade Skills available through the Gemini sidebar in Chrome include ways to maximize protein in recipes or summarize YouTube videos.

title: Physicists think they've resolved the proton size puzzle
description: "We believe this is the final nail in the coffin of the proton radius puzzle."

title: NASA chose the right crew to launch a new era of human space exploration
description: "It’s a special thing to be human, and it’s a special thing to be on planet Earth."

title: Google will begin punishing sites for back button hijacking in June
description: Google says it could penalize back button hijacking by demoting websites in search ranking.

title: IONNA Rechargeries are coming to more than 350 Circle K stations
description: The OEM-backed charging network offers 400 kW NACS and CCS DC fast chargers.

title: In just a couple weeks, StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
description:
The first StrictlyVC of the year will be coming to San Francisco before you know it.

There are still a few tickets available to join us and our stacked speaker lineup April 30, so register today!

title: Anthropic Opposes the Extreme AI Liability Bill That OpenAI Backed
description: Anthropic and OpenAI are clashing over a proposed Illinois law that would let AI labs largely off the hook for mass deaths and financial disasters.

title: Google brings its Gemini Personal Intelligence feature to India
description: The feature lets users connect Google accounts like Gmail and Photos to get personalized answers.

title: How vibe-coding app Anything is rebuilding after getting booted from the App Store twice
description: Anything is planning to launch a desktop companion app to aid mobile app development after its App Store removal.

description: Each year we compile our 10 Breakthrough Technologies list, featuring our educated predictions for which technologies will have the biggest impact on how we live and work. title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own
description: Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee, helped pass one of the country’s toughest AI laws. Now Silicon Valley’s biggest names are trying to stop his rise to Congress.

title: The problem with thinking you’re part Neanderthal
description: You’ve probably heard some version of this idea before: that many of us have an “inner Neanderthal.” That is to say, around 45,000 years ago, when Homo sapiens first arrived in Europe, they met members of a cousin species—the broad-browed, heavier-set Neanderthals—and, well, one thing led to another

title: OpenAI has bought AI personal finance startup Hiro
description: The acquisition indicates a capability that OpenAI is building into ChatGPT: financial planning.

title: You Can Soon Buy a $4,370 Humanoid Robot on AliExpress
description: Unitree is bringing its R1 to international markets. It arrives with some aerobatic capabilities and an entry-level price, but the question of what you'd actually do with it remains open.

title: Retro Rewind re-creates the glorious drudgery of working a '90s video store
description: What the nostalgic throwback lacks in complexity it makes up for in repetitive charm.

title: Meta Is Warned That Facial Recognition Glasses Will Arm Sexual Predators
description: More than 70 organizations, including the ACLU, EPIC, and Fight for the Future, say the AI smart glasses feature would endanger abuse victims, immigrants, and LGBTQ+ people.

description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
